description: Create a content slide with the headline on building resilience as Chinese high-tech competition and trade dynamics evolve, using a full-width table that compares four scenarios and strategic considerations across commercial, supply chain, and finance/risk.

header.headline: Companies need to build resilience under any scenario by being able adjust course as Chinese high-tech competition and trade dynamics evolve

headers: What would need to be true?
rows: Steady rise in Chinese high-tech exports, pricing pressure, but no major policy moves
Demand broadly stable; Trade policy steady, with China focused on mid-range cost competition
Tilt mix toward defensible niches and service-led offerings in exposed lines
Execute "no-regrets" efficiency moves (e.g., yield, procurement, basic China+1 options)
Build integrated scenarios by product and region, with early-warning triggers on profitability and cash
Targeted tariffs and trade remedies in a few product areas offer partial shelter
US/EU apply sector-specific remedies; Retaliation remains contained
Reposition portfolio to benefit from protected segments where you have a right to win
Adjust footprint to capture local-content incentives in priority markets
Model tariff, subsidy and local-content scenarios into P&L, balance sheet and capex plans
